interactions.summary: Jiang closes the Roman lecture by restating Rome as an empire based on hate, slavery, debt, and corruption, and sets up Jesus as the poet-prophet response who will try to redeem people.

claims.claim: The Roman Empire is summarized as an empire based on hate, terrible lives, massive slavery, debt, and corruption.
Jesus is introduced as the next poet-prophet who emerges to redeem people under the Roman Empire.

signature_moments.moment: The close restores the lecture’s opening thesis in compressed form.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Frames the entire episode as preparation for the Jesus lecture.

signature_moments.moment: Jesus enters as response to Roman degradation.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Connects Rome’s anti-civilization to the next source in the chronology.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Here applied to Jesus as a redemptive figure emerging after Roman degradation.

chronology_notes.note: Explicit handoff to the next class on Jesus as the response to Rome.
